constraints: Chef has received a mathematics assignment that requires array transformations.
Given an array AAA of size NNN and a positive integer MMM, perform these steps:
First, create array BBB such that Bi=Ai×M! ;B_i = A_i \times M!\ ;Bi​=Ai​×M! ; (M!(M!(M! denotes the factorial of M)M)M).
Then, for each element in array BBB, calculate F(Bi)F(B_i)F(Bi​), where F(x)F(x)F(x) represents the number of divisors of xxx.
Since these values can be large, output each F(Bi)F(B_i)F(Bi​) modulo (109+7)(10^9 + 7)(109+7).
The first line of input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, as mentioned in the statement.
The second line of input contains NNN space-separated integers, denoting the elements of array AAA .
Output NNN space-separated integers representing F(Bi)F(B_i)F(Bi​) modulo (109+7)(10^9 + 7)(109+7).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The initial array A=[1,2,3]A = [1, 2, 3]A=[1,2,3] and M=3M = 3M=3. Thus M!=3⋅2⋅1=6M! = 3\cdot 2\cdot 1 = 6M!=3⋅2⋅1=6.
The array B=[1⋅6,2⋅6,3⋅6]=[6,12,18]B = [1\cdot 6, 2\cdot 6, 3\cdot 6] = [6, 12, 18]B=[1⋅6,2⋅6,3⋅6]=[6,12,18].
The number of divisors of 6,12,6, 12,6,12, and 181818 are 4,6,4, 6,4,6, and 666 respectively.
